

```{r}
#===============================================================================
# Interaction Plots: Divergent vs. Parallel Climate Adaptation
# Intermediate Wheatgrass (Thinopyrum intermedium)
#
# Author: Elizabeth Campbell
# Affiliation: Department of Tropical Plant and Soil Sciences,
#              University of Hawaii at Manoa
# Date: 1/9/2026
#
# Description: Creates schematic interaction plots illustrating divergent
#              (population-specific) vs. parallel (universal) adaptation
#              patterns in climate-associated SNPs.
#
# Figure: Shows genotype × population interactions
#   Panel A: Divergent adaptation (24.2% of associations)
#   Panel B: Parallel adaptation (75.8% of associations)
#===============================================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(dplyr)

# Setup
BASE_DIR <- "C:/Users/Lizzy/OneDrive/Documents/UH_Manoa_Classes/IWG_Chapter_2"
OUTPUT_DIR <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations/Figures")
dir.create(OUTPUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

set.seed(42)  # Reproducibility

#===============================================================================
# PANEL A: DIVERGENT ADAPTATION (SJ02_159395471 × BIO13)
# Most significant G×P interaction (q = 5.2×10⁻⁹, R² = 34.0%)
#===============================================================================

# Climate range: Precipitation wettest month (50-200 mm)
climate_range <- seq(50, 200, length.out = 50)

# Six populations with DIFFERENT slopes (divergent)
populations_div <- list(
  "Pop 1" = list(slope = 0.0025, intercept = 0.3, color = "#e41a1c"),
  "Pop 2" = list(slope = 0.0010, intercept = 0.45, color = "#377eb8"),
  "Pop 3" = list(slope = 0.0035, intercept = 0.2, color = "#4daf4a"),
  "Pop 4" = list(slope = 0.0015, intercept = 0.5, color = "#984ea3"),
  "Pop 5" = list(slope = 0.0005, intercept = 0.55, color = "#ff7f00"),
  "Pop 6" = list(slope = -0.0005, intercept = 0.6, color = "#a65628")
)

# Generate data
data_div <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(populations_div), function(pop) {
  params <- populations_div[[pop]]
  freq <- params$slope * climate_range + params$intercept
  freq <- freq + rnorm(length(climate_range), 0, 0.03)
  freq <- pmax(0, pmin(1, freq))
  
  sample_idx <- sample(1:length(climate_range), 8)
  
  data.frame(
    climate = climate_range,
    allele_freq = freq,
    population = pop,
    color = params$color,
    is_point = 1:length(climate_range) %in% sample_idx
  )
}))

# Plot Panel A
panel_a <- ggplot(data_div, aes(x = climate, y = allele_freq, color = population)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(data = data_div[data_div$is_point, ], size = 2.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(sapply(populations_div, function(x) x$color), 
                                       names(populations_div))) +
  labs(
    x = "Precipitation Wettest Month (mm)",
    y = "Allele Frequency",
    title = "A. Divergent Adaptation: SJ02_159395471",
    subtitle = expression(paste("Significant G×P interaction (q = 5.2×10"^-9*", R² = 34.0%)"))
  ) +
  ylim(-0.05, 1.05) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.15, 0.25),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 180, y = 0.95, 
           label = "Different slopes indicate\npopulation-specific effects",
           size = 3.5, hjust = 1)

#===============================================================================
# PANEL B: PARALLEL ADAPTATION (SS01_212548100 × BIO15)
# Genome-wide significant (p = 7.6×10⁻⁹, R² = 10.4%), no G×P interaction (q = 0.19)
#===============================================================================

# Climate range: Precipitation seasonality (30-95% CV)
climate_range2 <- seq(30, 95, length.out = 50)

# Six populations with SAME slope (parallel)
populations_par <- list(
  "Pop 1" = list(slope = 0.0080, intercept = 0.15, color = "#e41a1c"),
  "Pop 2" = list(slope = 0.0080, intercept = 0.25, color = "#377eb8"),
  "Pop 3" = list(slope = 0.0080, intercept = 0.10, color = "#4daf4a"),
  "Pop 4" = list(slope = 0.0080, intercept = 0.35, color = "#984ea3"),
  "Pop 5" = list(slope = 0.0080, intercept = 0.30, color = "#ff7f00"),
  "Pop 6" = list(slope = 0.0080, intercept = 0.20, color = "#a65628")
)

# Generate data
data_par <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(populations_par), function(pop) {
  params <- populations_par[[pop]]
  freq <- params$slope * climate_range2 + params$intercept
  freq <- freq + rnorm(length(climate_range2), 0, 0.03)
  freq <- pmax(0, pmin(1, freq))
  
  sample_idx <- sample(1:length(climate_range2), 8)
  
  data.frame(
    climate = climate_range2,
    allele_freq = freq,
    population = pop,
    color = params$color,
    is_point = 1:length(climate_range2) %in% sample_idx
  )
}))

# Plot Panel B
panel_b <- ggplot(data_par, aes(x = climate, y = allele_freq, color = population)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(data = data_par[data_par$is_point, ], size = 2.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(sapply(populations_par, function(x) x$color), 
                                       names(populations_par))) +
  labs(
    x = "Precipitation Seasonality (Coefficient of Variation, %)",
    y = "Allele Frequency",
    title = "B. Parallel Adaptation: SS01_212548100",
    subtitle = expression(paste("No G×P interaction (q = 0.19), genome-wide sig. (p = 7.6×10"^-9*", R² = 10.4%)"))
  ) +
  ylim(-0.05, 1.05) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.15, 0.25),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 90, y = 0.25, 
           label = "Parallel slopes indicate\nuniversal adaptation",
           size = 3.5, hjust = 1)

#===============================================================================
# COMBINE AND SAVE
#===============================================================================

combined_plot <- arrangeGrob(panel_a, panel_b, ncol = 2)

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_Interaction_Plots.png"), combined_plot, 
       width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_Interaction_Plots.pdf"), combined_plot, 
       width = 14, height = 6, bg = "white")

cat("\n✓ Interaction plots created successfully!\n")
cat("  Divergent adaptation: 24.2% (significant G×P interactions)\n")
cat("  Parallel adaptation: 75.8% (universal effects)\n")
cat("  Files saved to:", OUTPUT_DIR, "\n\n")

#===============================================================================
# END
#===============================================================================
```
